LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT CORPORATE BUILDERS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT: GRANTED PER NONDISCRETIONARY STATUTE. THE COURT'S ORDER ENTERED FEBRUARY 3, 2004 IS MODIFIED NON PRO TUNC TO DELETE THE REQUIREMENT THAT CORPORATE BUILDERS, INC. PAY TO PLAINTIFF ROSENDIN ELEICTRIC, INC. THE SUM OF $5,135. THE SUM OF $5,135.00 IS ASSESSED AGAINST WILLIAM F. HERTZ PERSONALLY. COUNSEL WILLIAM F. HERTZ SHALL ALSO COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S PRIOR ORDER OF 11/26/03 ASSESSING THE SUM OF $1,100.00 AS SANCTIONS. WILLIAM F. HERTZ SHALL PAY THE SUM OF $5,135.00 AND $1,100.00 TO PLAINTIFF ROSENDIN ELECTRIC, INC. NO LATER THAN 5 PM ON 02/17/04. ATTORNEY ROSENBERG WILL SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CRC 391. JUDGE: JAMES L. WARREN REPORTER: CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR #1558

calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-10-2004 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kenneth G. Barth, Esq. (858) 558-0497 specially appearing on behalf of William F. Hertz II for Defendant and Moving Party Corporate Builders Inc.
Patricia G. Rosenberg, Esq., Haas & Najarian (415) 788-6330 for Plaintiff and Opposing Party Rosendin Electric, Inc.
RULING:
DEFENDANT CORPORATE BUILDERS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT: GRANTED per nondiscretionary statute. The Court's order entered February 3, 2004 is modified non pro tunc to delete the requirement that Corporate Builders, Inc. pay to Plaintiff Rosendin Eleictric, Inc. the sum of $5,135. The sum of $5,135.00 is assessed against William F. Hertz personally. Counsel William F. Hertz shall also comply with the Court's prior order of 11/26/03 assessing the sum of $1,100.00 as sanctions. William F. Hertz shall pay the sum of $5,135.00 and $1,100.00 to Plaintiff Rosendin Electric, Inc. no later than 5 PM on 02/17/04. Attorney Rosenberg will submit a form of order pursuant to CRC 391. (301/JLW/EH) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558

Motion To Reinstate Default Judgment And Compel Deft Corporate Builder'S Compliance With The Aug.19,2003 Court Order And Request For Osc
Off Calendar Nov-26-2003 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Arthur Laughton, Esq., Specially Appearing on behalf of William Hertz, Esq. (650) 533-8228, for Defendant Corporate Builders, Inc., opposing motion

RULING:
Attorney Laughton did not notify Plaintiff's counsel of his appearance today. PLAINTIFF ROSENDIN ELECTRIC, INC.'S Motion To Reinstate Default Judgment And Compel Defendant Corporate Builder's Compliance With The Aug.19,2003 Court Order And Request For OSC: GRANTED; the opposition was filed late on November 21, 2003. Prevailing party to prepare order. (301/JLW) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558

MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCE: MAYA R. MOISEYEV, 415-260-2487, SPECIALY APPEARING FOR WILLIAM HERTZ FOR DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY, CORPORATE BUILDERS, INC.

THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING.
DEFENDANT CORPORATE BUILDERS, INC.'S Motion To Vacate And/Or Set Aside Default And/Or Default Judgment Pursuant To And Under Ccp 473 -- Granted. Plaintiff entitled to attorney's fees and costs in the amount of $5,135.50 within 10 days of entry of the order.
JUDGE: JAMES L. WARREN, Reporter: Christina Paxton CSR #1558 (JLW/301/EH)

Appearances:
Patricia G. Rosenberg, Esq. for Plaintiff Rosendin Electric, Inc. Mark Lynch, Authorized agent, sworn and examined.

Total: $60,892.72
